resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: This is an availability statement. It does not diagnose rheumatologic, autoimmune, arthritis, lupus, or inflammatory disease; estimate disease risk or disease activity; interpret labs; infer flare status; recommend medication, immunosuppression, supplements, diet, screening, testing, or treatment; or replace rheumatologist review.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sibling Immunity & Inflammation sample shows joint inflammation, gut inflammation, eczema, psoriasis, likelihood labels, percentiles, impact/evidence ratings, and visible variant tables. These rows are used only to preserve output shape for Rheumatologist Summary.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: The sample rows show what a report format can contain. They are not local findings and must not be read as personal rheumatology diagnosis, autoimmune disease risk, arthritis risk, lupus status, inflammatory disease activity, flare status, lab interpretation, treatment need, supplement need, diet need, screening need, testing need, or actionability.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The local fixture intentionally omits the clinical, lab, imaging, medication, and professional context needed for rheumatology decisions, so the output marks those areas unavailable instead of inferring them.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Rheumatology decisions need symptoms, exam findings, imaging, lab results, medications, family history, disease activity context, flare context, and qualified review. Missing context is reported as missing, not normal, abnormal, low risk, high risk, diagnostic, actionable, or all clear.

professionalHandoffContext.professionalReview: Use this output only as preparation for a rheumatologist conversation. Health decisions require qualified review with symptoms, exam findings, imaging, lab results, medication context, family history, disease activity, flare context, and validated testing scope.

professionalReview: Do not use this local fixture to change medication, supplements, diet, screening, testing, prevention plans, or treatment decisions.

Bring symptoms, joint exam findings, imaging, RF, anti-CCP, ANA, anti-dsDNA, complement tests, CRP, ESR, organ involvement notes, medications, family history, and flare context to a qualified professional if discussing this topic.
Treat sibling Immunity & Inflammation sample rows as report-format examples only, not as local rheumatology findings.
